rule: Every mechanical constant used in any R-module code must appear here with verbatim quoted_text from the canonical source. sim_fabrication_check enforces this at commit.

modules_verified.name: R1 Wound permanence
modules_verified.decision: C1.3 (Knot-tagged via existing Conviction Scar channel)
rejected_candidates: C1.2 catastrophic-permanent (categorical R+S fail at full NERS)
modules_verified.open_finding: Pool floor 5D is load-bearing for permanent-wound asymmetry; Threadwork penalty unfloored is independent stress-test candidate; fieldwork §3 line 364 has stale PP-684 citation needing post-renumber update
modules_verified.superseded: 2026-05-09 by PP-716 (commit 6e3a8ae) — built on stale Vitality formula and unfloored +1 Ob Threadwork penalty

modules_verified.name: R1 Wound permanence (PP-716 canon)
modules_verified.decision: C1.3 (Knot-tagged via existing Conviction Scar channel) — UNCHANGED from v1
modules_verified.rationale_change_under_pp716: v1 cited practitioner-extinction cascade as central rejection of C1.2 (built on +1 Ob Threadwork unfloored). PP-716 reverts to −1D Pool with floor 5D, removing the cascade. C1.2 still rejects, but for ambiguity ('catastrophic' undefined) and direct canon contradiction (R1v2-L07) rather than mechanical cascade. C1.3 recommendation is robust to the canon correction.
modules_verified.open_finding: Pool asymmetry persists but is bounded by MW under PP-716 (worst case 12D → 8D at End=7 carry vs 5D at End=1 floor — 3D gap, halved from v1's 7D gap)

modules_verified.name: R2 Skill input layer in Architecture C
modules_verified.decision: C2.1 (pure dice, cognitive only) — primary; C2.2 (cap +1 net hit) — fallback if genre-expectation gap is unacceptable
rejected_candidates: C2.3 cap +2 (marginal — stat relevance halved at low pool, cross-system asymmetry)
C2.4 uncapped (categorical multi-axis fail — tabletop-incompatible R2-L02, inverts stat hierarchy)
modules_verified.open_finding: Pirates! E5 + Sekiro E7 already adopted carry C-distinctiveness without motor-skill layer. Genre-expectation gap acknowledged as Horizontal ⚠ but not categorical.

modules_verified.name: R3 Mass-battle three-mode reframe
modules_verified.decision: C3.3 (preserve canonical structure + add phase-mapping table to top of mass_battle_v30)
rejected_candidates: C3.2 cosmetic rename (high propagation cost for low value)
modules_verified.key_finding: Synthesis premise partially obsolete: v30 canon already merged TTRPG/Hybrid (R3-L01, R3-L07) on 2026-04-17. R3 re-asked against actual structure: no mechanical restructure needed. Documentation addition (I-tier ↔ Part phase-mapping) is the only output needed.

modules_verified.name: R4 Hero participation default
modules_verified.decision: C4.2 (default-off, Tier 1 strategic baseline; Tier 2 scene-scale opt-in via §B.5 phase-lock)
rejected_candidates: C4.1 default-on (categorical S-fail: contradicts §B.5 phase-lock framing R4-L01; E-fail: per-battle overhead; starves named-officer Disposition channel R4-L03)
modules_verified.deferred_refinement: C4.3 context-sensitive — adopt only after Q5 T-trigger criteria (T-Boss / T-Cultural / T-Honor-call) formally specified (P1 ambiguity per EC-D4.A-01)
modules_verified.key_finding: Three-tier presence model formalized: Tier 0 absent (BG + Where-Were-You), Tier 1 present-strategic (commander, default), Tier 2 present-scene (Zoom In opt-in). §D.3 player wound morale shock R4-L05 designed for exceptional Tier 2 events.

modules_verified.name: R5 Wager stake range
modules_verified.decision: C5.1 (preserve canonical Wager Obligation per §6.1.1 — stake severity structurally derived from contest type per R5-L01, not from a parallel tag)
rejected_candidates: C5.2 stake-severity tag (functionally redundant with contest-type hierarchy)
modules_verified.deferred_refinement: C5.3 voluntary stake escalation — genuine player-agency value but substantial cross-system coupling cost; recommend separate design initiative if pursued
modules_verified.key_finding: Synthesis premise partially obsolete: canon's Wager Obligation post-ED-778 is mature; stake severity encoded structurally via contest type (Formal/Grand/Royal/Tribunal) rather than as parallel tag. Cosmetic-stake gap (tavern-game / dueling-purse content) belongs to fieldwork-Socializing, not Wager.

modules_verified.name: R6 Fibonacci cap at high N
modules_verified.decision: C6.1 (preserve canonical +5 cap at 8+ attackers per R6-L01; PP-216)
rejected_candidates: C6.3 no cap (categorical multi-axis fail — defender Pool relevance breaks at 13+ attackers)
C6.4 asymptotic decay (E ✗ and S ✗ — fractional dice contradicts canonical integer-dice convention)
modules_verified.marginal: C6.2 raise cap to +7 at 21+ — mechanically functional but solves a rare problem at moderate spec/propagation cost
modules_verified.key_finding: Fibonacci bonus +5 cap (Δ-net +1.65 at saturation) produces tactically significant attacker advantage without breaking defender Pool relevance. Uncapped at 21 attackers (Δ-net +2.31) drowns 12D defender. Mass-scale uncapped at 100+ attackers (Δ-net +4 from bonus alone) collapses canonical resolution math entirely.

modules_verified.name: R7 Friendly fire & ranged in melee
modules_verified.decision: C7.4 (FF on stress only — ≥1 Wound, Composure ≤3, or environmental degradation) primary; C7.1 (no FF, preserve canon-silence) fallback if stress-spec work not justified
rejected_candidates: C7.3 separate d10 roll (non-canonical dice channel; stylistically inconsistent with canonical pool-only resolution)
modules_verified.marginal: C7.2 FF on every miss — functionally workable but per-miss adjudication overhead; high-frequency mechanic
modules_verified.spec_gap: Stress threshold criteria need explicit enumeration (P1 ambiguity per EC-D7.A-01): Wound count, Composure threshold, environmental degradation list. Spec gap is filled by Jordan ratification per PP.

modules_verified.name: R8 Fieldwork ↔ combat tempo shift
rejected_candidates: C8.1 full freeze (S-fail: contradicts Exposure / Evidence propagation R8-L01/L05)
C8.3 continuous tempo (categorical multi-axis fail: contradicts R8-L02 battle-time exclusion; dual-clock bookkeeping; time-laundering optimization)
modules_verified.key_finding: Synthesis premise was a spec-gap that doesn't exist: combat_v30 §11.5 specifies the tempo model completely. Partial-freeze with one-way state coupling (Exposure propagates forward at entry; Evidence persists post-combat with seasonal decay; fieldwork state persists across combat with state-preserving resumption) is canonical and mature. R8 answered by re-pointing to canon.

modules_verified.name: R9 Two-architecture sufficiency
modules_verified.decision: C9.1 (A+C only — synthesis Q5 reduction holds) primary; C9.3 (A+C with optional B-shape presentation flag) as UX refinement if Jordan wants explicit corridor / fixed-position presentation contrast
rejected_candidates: C9.2 restore B (high cost, low marginal value — three combat UIs, B-as-orphan-mid-tier doesn't extend to mass)
modules_verified.key_finding: B-shaped encounter coverage entirely replicable by A+S7-hybrid via zone-size + actor-count + Stunt-vocabulary parameters. Mapping verified for: corridor ambush, dungeon room, tavern brawl, field battle, hero+lieutenant+mooks. Variety-via-architecture forfeit (C9.1's 1⚠ Horizontal) is mitigated by variety-via-zone-parameters.

constants.quoted_text: Combat Pool = (Agility × 2) + Relevant History + 3 (minimum 5)
Each Wound: −1D Combat Pool only (cumulative). No Ob penalty from wounds.

constants.value: +1 Ob per Wound, all Thread ops
constants.quoted_text: - **Wound penalties:** +1 Ob per Wound to all Thread operations (threadwork_v30 §2.3).
**Vitality = Endurance × 10** (range 10–70). Total damage capacity. Equipment adds flat Vitality. Incapacitated at 0.
**Wound Interval = Endurance + 6** (range 7–13). Wounds accrue at floor(cumulative_damage / Wound_Interval).
Session end: all wounds clear
Stabilised characters may return to action after one full scene of rest. Wounds clear at end of session (or after extended rest per GM).
Combat Pool minimum 5 is a clean floor. No −1D penalty at the floor.

constants.value: permanence channel exists, attached to Conviction not Wound
constants.quoted_text: Lifetime; shifts only through Scar accumulation or major narrative events
| Distant Knot | 4 strain | Can be upgraded to Close (per §5.6a Success outcome).

constants.value: +1 Wound to Knot partner on FR Dissolution
constants.quoted_text: Tears the Knot directly; partner-of-rupture takes +1 Wound (no armor).

constants.value: scar on all witnesses to Dissolution
constants.quoted_text: **Dissolution in combat:** RS cost per threadwork_v30 §5.2. Scar on all witnesses per npc_behavior_v30 §3.4.
**Coherence** (survival resource): unchanged. 10→0 countdown. Not a derived value — a permanent degradation track.
Combat Pool = (Agility × 2) + Relevant History + 3 (minimum 5)
Each Wound: −1D Combat Pool only (cumulative). No Ob penalty from wounds.

constants.value: −1D to ALL Pools (Combat, Thread, mass-Command) per Wound
constants.quoted_text: Wound penalty | −1D to ALL Pools (Combat, Thread — Leap, Weaving, Pulling, Mending, FR — Hybrid mass-battle Command). Universal rule.
Formula | `Health (full) = (Endurance + 6) × (Max Wounds + 1)` = WI × (MW+1)
Max Wounds | `Max Wounds = floor(Endurance / 2) + 1`
Wound Interval | `WI = Endurance + 6` (range 7–13 across End 1–7)

constants.value: All wounds clear at session end
constants.quoted_text: Wounds clearance | All wounds clear at session end (canonical). Stabilised characters return to action after one full scene of rest.
Felled (incapacitated) at 0 Health, which equals MW + 1 wounds accrued.

constants.value: minimum 5D, no further wound penalty at floor

Feint | Player chooses commit: allocate N dice (minimum 3) to Offence for the feint

constants.value: Existing Game-Master-judged skill modifier with cap +5

Base TN = 7

constants.value: Same dice engine as personal combat
constants.quoted_text: Mass battle uses the same dice engine as personal combat.
**Effective Combat Pool = min(Size, Command) + Command** (PP-233)

constants.value: BG resolves in single Priority 2 slot, 3-5 min total
constants.quoted_text:
BG battles resolve in a single Priority 2 slot. Total resolution time: 3–5
minutes.

constants.value: Strategic depth in preparation, not execution
constants.quoted_text:
Strategic depth lives in preparation (unit composition, order
choice, tactic card selection), not in turn-by-turn execution.

constants.value: Three legal phase-lock points for Zoom In
constants.quoted_text:
Zoom In may only fire at one of three legal phase-lock points:
- **After Phase 1** (orders placed, nothing resolved — cleanest entry)
- **After Phase 3** (manoeuvre complete, pre-Engagement)
- **After Phase 6 Step 1** (all damage applied, no ghost units)
Status: CANONICAL — approved 2026-04-17 (editorial batch acceptance)

constants.value: Zoom In may only fire at three legal phase-lock points
constants.quoted_text:
Zoom In may only fire at one of three legal phase-lock points:
- **After Phase 1** (orders placed, nothing resolved — cleanest entry)
- **After Phase 3** (manoeuvre complete, pre-Engagement)
- **After Phase 6 Step 1** (all damage applied, no ghost units)

**If the player was not present for the battle:** The aftermath scene is replaced by a "Where Were You?" retrospective scene per scale_transitions_v30 §4.4. The player learns about the battle's outcome through their social network. The three aftermath choices are not available — the moment has passed.

constants.value: Officer Disposition shifts based on player tactical command quality
constants.quoted_text:
- Player issues a tactically sound command (Battle turn where player's choice contributes to victory): +1
- Player orders a retreat or sacrifice that saves the unit: +1
- Player orders the unit into a situation that costs Size ≥ 2: −1
Units in the player's current territory gain +1 Discipline while the player is physically present during battle.
If the player is wounded (2+ wounds) or incapacitated during the battle, all friendly units in the battle take −1 Discipline immediately (morale shock).

constants.value: Personal combat pause: Command suspension and Discipline bonus independent
constants.quoted_text: This bonus applies whenever the player is in the territory, including during a personal combat pause (the player is still present; Command suspension and Discipline bonus are independent effects).

constants.value: Player Character not in battle: BG resolution fires
constants.quoted_text: **No Player Character in battle:** BG resolution fires.
## §3 Eight Handoff Rules

constants.value: Formal 2 seasons; Grand 4 seasons or until condition; Royal 2 seasons; Tribunal until revoked
constants.quoted_text: | Formal Contest (Parliament) | 2 seasons | Violating faction: Mandate −1. Reputation shift. Contest Fatigue on faction leader. |

constants.value: Mandate −2, Stability −1, +2 Ob next DA targeting violator
constants.quoted_text: **Condition not met within timeframe:** Obligation fails. Default Grand Contest violation consequences fire (Mandate −2, Stability −1, +2 Ob next DA targeting

constants.value: Conviction Scar accumulation through major narrative events
constants.quoted_text: Lifetime; shifts only through Scar accumulation or major narrative events
When 3+ combatants in a zone: combat becomes group combat. Fibonacci bonus applies.
Each additional attacker beyond the first against a single unsupported target adds dice to Offence allocation. (PP-216)
Base TN = 7

constants.value: Rescue eligible if outnumbered (subject to Fibonacci)
constants.quoted_text: **Eligibility (PP-290):** Rescue may only be declared if the rescued actor is outnumbered at Phase 1 declaration — facing 2+ attackers with no supporting ally
### Ranged Weapons (distinct category — ED-129 resolved)

constants.value: LP TN 7, HP TN 6, Sling TN 8
constants.quoted_text: | LP — Light Piercing (bow) | 7 | Min STR 2 |

constants.value: Close = Melee range; Far = Ranged distance
constants.quoted_text: **Zone terminology (PP-268):** Close zone renamed **Melee range**; Far zone renamed **Ranged distance** throughout.

constants.value: Ranged in Close zone defends at Def TN 8
constants.quoted_text: Ranged defence at Close zone: a character carrying a ranged weapon may defend at Def TN 8 if forced into Close zone by a melee attacker.
Net hits = Offence successes − Defence successes (minimum 0).
**Eligibility (PP-290):** Rescue may only be declared if the rescued actor is outnumbered at Phase 1 declaration — facing 2+ attackers with no supporting ally
Combat Exposure codified: quiet engagement +1, conspicuous +2, public +3 Exposure to the fieldwork-active character. Applied before the combat scene opens, not during it.

constants.value: Battle does not consume fieldwork time
constants.quoted_text: The battle itself does not consume fieldwork time; only the post-combat investigation does.

constants.value: Fleeing combat does NOT reset fieldwork state
constants.quoted_text: Fleeing combat does NOT reset the player's fieldwork state — any active investigation or social engagement in the territory continues from where it was when combat interrupted.

constants.value: Exposure from fled combat still applies
constants.quoted_text: Exposure from the fled combat still applies per F-TRANS-01/09 (quiet +1, conspicuous +2, public +3). Flight does not reduce Exposure — witnesses saw the player arrive, even if they left.
Post-combat investigation of battle site = 1 fieldwork scene.

constants.value: +1 Ob/season Evidence decay at fled-combat site
constants.quoted_text: Evidence degrades: +1 Ob to investigation per season elapsed since the battle.
## §6 EXPOSURE
Chunks 1–2 establish **A (map+move), B (slot formation), C (duel)** as three architectures.
Chunk 4 favors S7-hybrid which collapses A and B into the same spatial substrate (continuous within a zone). C remains specialized.

constants.value: Drop B as a distinct architecture
constants.quoted_text: **Drop B as a distinct architecture.** Use A (S7-hybrid) for all general combat — adjust *zone size, encounter density, animation speed* to span the use cases B would have served

constants.value: C fires on six discriminable conditions
constants.quoted_text: C fires on six discriminable conditions, all canon-adjacent. Default is A.
Combat Pool = (Agility × 2) + Relevant History + 3 (minimum 5)

constants.value: Close = Melee range; Far = Ranged distance
constants.quoted_text: **Zone terminology (PP-268):** Close zone renamed **Melee range**; Far zone renamed **Ranged distance** throughout.

self_audit_corrections_active: NERS at full grain (24 cells per candidate option, no compression)
N-failures categorical (any N=fail rejects outright, no vote-counting)
Diagonal vs lateral boundary fixed: diagonal=cross-system, lateral=same-scale parallel
No threshold drift: per-direction scoring criteria fixed at module start
